main.description: An immutable snapshot of a document body. Each save creates a new version; the parent document record's currentVersion is updated to point here.

document.description: AT-URI of the fyi.requested.document this version belongs to. Not a strongRef because the document record mutates over time (currentVersion changes on every edit) — pinning to a CID would always be stale.

content.description: The document body as an embedded at.markpub.markdown object (markpub.at lexicon), making the markdown portable across the markpub ecosystem. The raw markdown source is content.text.markdown. Rendered client-side; no embedded HTML is trusted.

body.description: DEPRECATED. Legacy raw markdown source, present only on versions written before the markpub migration. New versions write `content` instead. Readers fall back to this field when `content` is absent.

previousVersion.description: Strong ref to the prior version, forming a chain. Absent on the first version. Versions are immutable so a CID-pinned ref is stable.
